accept: Cap value is documented in 'act close --help' and 'act help workflow' (the canonical-loop section), with the rationale (forces single-line audit summaries) or a pointer to the spec section if raised.
Either (a) cap is preserved and documented, or (b) cap is raised to match description's 16384 with rationale recorded in the spec.
Error message points to the doc location: 'op: close.reason length N > 500; see act close --help'.
payload.description: Discovered 2026-05-10 trying to close act-da03 with a 779-char reason: 'op: close.reason length 779 > 500'. The cap is enforced but not surfaced anywhere a caller would look: not in 'act close --help', not in 'act help workflow', not in spec-v2.md (the 16384 length cap is for description, not reason). Agents writing detailed audit-trail closes have no way to know about the limit until they hit it. Either raise the cap (since reasons are audit-bearing free text) or document it everywhere relevant.
payload.title: act close --reason has undocumented 500-char cap
